title: Equivalent ratios in the real world
description: This exercise goes beyond solving for equivalent ratios and gets at what it means for ratios to be equivalent.
topic_path: Equivalent ratios in the real world

assessment_items.question_content:
Patrick's favorite shade of purple paint is made with $4$ milliliters of blue paint for every $3$ milliliters of red paint.  

**Which of the following paint mixtures will create the same shade of purple?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The paint mixture will create the same shade of purple if the ratio of blue to red paint is equivalent to $4 : 3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Blue paint ($\text{mL}$) | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$ | $8$
Red paint ($\text{mL})$ | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$  | $6$

If the mixture has $2$ times as much blue paint, it also needs $2$ times as much red paint.

$8:6$ is equivalent to $4 : 3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Blue paint ($\text{mL}$) | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$ | $20$
Red paint ($\text{mL})$ | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$  | $15$

If the mixture has $5$ times as much blue paint, it also needs $5$ times as much red paint.

$20:15$ is equivalent to $4 : 3$.

The following mixtures will create Patrick's favorite shade of purple:

- $8$ milliliters of blue paint mixed with $6$ milliliters of red paint
- $20$ milliliters of blue paint mixed with $15$ milliliters of red paint

choices.content: $3$ milliliters of blue paint mixed with $4$ milliliters of red paint
$8$ milliliters of blue paint mixed with $6$ milliliters of red paint
$6$ milliliters of blue paint mixed with $8$ milliliters of red paint
$20$ milliliters of blue paint mixed with $15$ milliliters of red paint
$12$ milliliters of blue paint mixed with $16$ milliliters of red paint

assessment_items.question_content:
Super Salad Dressing is made with $8\text{ mL}$ of oil for every $3\text{ mL}$ of vinegar.  

**Which of the following mixtures will taste the same as Super Salad Dressing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The mixture will have the same taste if the ratio of oil to vinegar  is equivalent to $8:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Oil ($\text{mL}$) | $8$ | $\stackrel{\times 6}\rightarrow$ | $48$
Vinegar ($\text{mL})$ | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 6}\rightarrow$  | $18$

If the mixture has $6$ times as much oil, it also needs $6$ times as much vinegar.

$48:18$ is equivalent to $8:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Oil ($\text{mL}$) | $8$ | $\stackrel{\times 7}\rightarrow$ | $56$
Vinegar ($\text{mL})$ | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 7}\rightarrow$  | $21$

If the mixture has $7$ times as much oil, it also needs $7$ times as much vinegar.

$56:21$ is equivalent to $8:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Oil ($\text{mL}$) | $8$ | $\stackrel{\times 10}\rightarrow$ | $80$
Vinegar ($\text{mL})$ | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 10}\rightarrow$  | $30$

If the mixture has $10$ times as much oil, it also needs $10$ times as much vinegar.

$80:30$ is equivalent to $8:3$.

The following mixtures will taste the same as Super Salad Dressing:

- $48\text{ mL}$ of oil with $18\text { mL}$ of vinegar
- $56\text{ mL}$ of oil with $21\text { mL}$ of vinegar
- $80\text{ mL}$ of oil with $30\text { mL}$ of vinegar

choices.content: $80\text{ mL}$ of oil with $30\text { mL}$ of vinegar
$3\text{ mL}$ of oil with $8\text { mL}$ of vinegar
$56\text{ mL}$ of oil with $21\text { mL}$ of vinegar
$40\text{ mL}$ of oil with $25\text { mL}$ of vinegar
$48\text{ mL}$ of oil with $18\text { mL}$ of vinegar

assessment_items.question_content:
A gray crayon is  made with $5\text{ mL}$ of black wax for every $6\text{ mL}$ of white wax.  

**Which of the following wax mixtures will create the same shade of gray?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The wax mixture will create the same shade of gray if the ratio of black to white paint is equivalent to $5:6$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Black wax ($\text{mL}$) | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$ | $15$
White wax ($\text{mL})$ | $6$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$  | $18$

If the mixture has $3$ times as much black wax, it also needs $3$ times as much white wax.

$15:18$ is equivalent to $5:6$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Black wax ($\text{mL}$) | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 7}\rightarrow$ | $35$
White wax ($\text{mL})$ | $6$ | $\stackrel{\times 7}\rightarrow$  | $42$

If the mixture has $7$ times as much black wax, it also needs $7$ times as much white wax.

$35:42$ is equivalent to $5:6$.

The following mixtures will create the same shade of gray:

- $15\text{ mL}$ of black wax mixed with $18\text { mL}$ of white wax
- $35\text{ mL}$ of black wax mixed with $42\text { mL}$ of white wax

choices.content: $10\text{ mL}$ of black wax mixed with $24\text { mL}$ of white wax
$15\text{ mL}$ of black wax mixed with $18\text { mL}$ of white wax
$35\text{ mL}$ of black wax mixed with $42\text { mL}$ of white wax
$20\text{ mL}$ of black wax mixed with $26\text { mL}$ of white wax
$45\text{ mL}$ of black wax mixed with $48\text { mL}$ of white wax

assessment_items.question_content:
Flaming BBQ Restaurant makes a dipping sauce with $9\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce for every $6$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce.  

**Which of the following mixtures will taste the same as Flaming BBQ's dipping sauce?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The mixture will have the same taste if the ratio of hot sauce to barbecue sauce is equivalent to $9:6$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Hot sauce ($\text{mL}$) | $9$ | $\stackrel{\div 3}\rightarrow$ | $3$
Barbecue sauce ($\text{mL})$ | $6$ | $\stackrel{\div 3}\rightarrow$  | $2$

If the mixture has a third of the amount of hot sauce, it also needs a third of the amount of barbecue sauce.

$3:2$ is equivalent to $9:6$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Hot sauce ($\text{mL}$) | $9$ | $\stackrel{\times 1\frac{1}{3}}\rightarrow$ | $12$
Barbecue sauce ($\text{mL})$ | $6$ | $\stackrel{\times 1\frac{1}{3}}\rightarrow$  | $8$

If the mixture has $1\dfrac13$ times as much hot sauce, it also needs $1\dfrac13$ times as much barbecue sauce.

$12:8$ is equivalent to $9:6$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Hot sauce ($\text{mL}$) | $9$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$ | $45$
Barbecue sauce ($\text{mL})$ | $6$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$  | $30$

If the mixture has $5$ times as much hot sauce, it also needs $5$ times as much barbecue sauce.

$45:30$ is equivalent to $9:6$.

The following mixtures will taste the same as Flaming BBQ's dipping sauce:

- $3\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $2$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce
- $12\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $8$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce
- $45\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $30$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce

choices.content: $6\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $10$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce
$3\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $2$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce
$45\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $30$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce
$24\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $18$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce
$12\text{ mL}$ of hot sauce mixed with $8$ spoonfuls of barbecue sauce

assessment_items.question_content:
A pink crayon is  made with $12\text{ mL}$ of red wax for every $5\text{ mL}$ of white wax.  

**Which of the following wax mixtures will create the same shade of pink?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The wax mixture will create the same shade of pink if the ratio of red to white paint is equivalent to $12:5$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Red wax ($\text{mL}$) | $12$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$ | $36$
White wax ($\text{mL})$ | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$  | $15$

If the mixture has $3$ times as much red wax, it also needs $3$ times as much white wax.

$36:15$ is equivalent to $12:5$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Red wax ($\text{mL}$) | $12$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$ | $60$
White wax ($\text{mL})$ | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$  | $25$

If the mixture has $5$ times as much red wax, it also needs $5$ times as much white wax.

$60:25$ is equivalent to $12:5$.

The following mixtures will create the same shade of pink:

- $36\text{ mL}$ of red wax mixed with $15\text { mL}$ of white wax
- $60\text{ mL}$ of red wax mixed with $25\text { mL}$ of white wax

choices.content: $10\text{ mL}$ of red wax mixed with $24\text { mL}$ of white wax
$48\text{ mL}$ of red wax mixed with $12\text { mL}$ of white wax
$13\text{ mL}$ of red wax mixed with $6\text { mL}$ of white wax
$36\text{ mL}$ of red wax mixed with $15\text { mL}$ of white wax
$60\text{ mL}$ of red wax mixed with $25\text { mL}$ of white wax

assessment_items.question_content:
Mr. Shen likes  for his homework assignments to have $2$ addition problems for every $3$ subtraction problems. 

**Which of the following homework assignments could Mr. Shen create?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The assignment will work if the ratio of addition problems to subtraction problems is $2:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Addition problems | $2$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$ | $6$
Subtraction problems | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$  | $9$

If the assignment has $3$ times as many addition problems, it also needs $3$ times as many subtraction problems.

$6:9$ is equivalent to $2:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Addition problems | $2$ | $\stackrel{\times 4}\rightarrow$ | $8$
Subtraction problems | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 4}\rightarrow$  | $12$

If the assignment has $4$ times as many addition problems, it also needs $4$ times as many subtraction problems.

$8:12$ is equivalent to $2:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Addition problems | $2$ | $\stackrel{\times 8}\rightarrow$ | $16$
Subtraction problems | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 8}\rightarrow$  | $24$

If the assignment has $8$ times as many addition problems, it also needs $8$ times as many subtraction problems.

$16:24$ is equivalent to $2:3$.

The following assignments will work:

- $6$ addition problems and $9$ subtraction problems
- $8$ addition problems and $12$ subtraction problems
- $16$ addition problems and $24$ subtraction problems

choices.content: $3$ addition problems and $4$ subtraction problems
$6$ addition problems and $9$ subtraction problems
$16$ addition problems and $24$ subtraction problems
$8$ addition problems and $12$ subtraction problems
$15$ addition problems and $10$ subtraction problems

assessment_items.question_content:
Julia exercises at the gym. For every $3$ minutes she spends running, she spends $5$ minutes lifting weights.

**Which of the following could be how Julia spends time at the gym?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Julia wants the ratio of time spent running to time spent lifting weights to be $3:5$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Running | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$ | $15$
Lifting | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$  | $25$

If she runs for $5$ times as long, then she also needs to lift for $5$ times as long.

$15:25$ is equivalent to $3:5$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Running | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 10}\rightarrow$ | $30$
Lifting | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 10}\rightarrow$  | $50$

If she runs for $10$ times as long, then she also needs to lift for $10$ times as long.

$30:50$ is equivalent to $3:5$.

Julia could spend time at the gym in the following ways:

- Run for $15$ minutes and lift weights for $25$ minutes
- Run for $30$ minutes and lift weights for $50$ minutes

choices.content: Run for $10$ minutes and lift weights for $12$ minutes
Run for $15$ minutes and lift weights for $25$ minutes
Run for $20$ minutes and lift weights for $30$ minutes
Run for $24$ minutes and lift weights for $48$ minutes
Run for $30$ minutes and lift weights for $50$ minutes

assessment_items.question_content:
Joseph is making kebabs. He wants each kebab to have $3$ pieces of meat and $4$ pieces of vegetables.

**Which of the following could Joseph use to make kebabs without any leftovers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Joseph wants the ratio of meat pieces to vegetable pieces to be $3:4$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Meat | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 10}\rightarrow$ | $30$
Vegetable | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 10}\rightarrow$  | $40$

If Joseph has $10$ times as many pieces of meat, then he needs $10$ times as many pieces of vegetables.

$30:40$ is equivalent to $3:4$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Meat | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 15}\rightarrow$ | $45$
Vegetable | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 15}\rightarrow$  | $60$

If Joseph has $15$ times as many pieces of meat, then he needs $15$ times as many pieces of vegetables.

$45:60$ is equivalent to $3:4$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Meat | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 24}\rightarrow$ | $72$
Vegetable | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 24}\rightarrow$  | $96$

If Joseph has $24$ times as many pieces of meat, then he needs $24$ times as many pieces of vegetables.

$72:96$ is equivalent to $3:4$.

Joseph could make kebabs with the following without any leftovers:

- $30$ pieces of meat and $40$ pieces of vegetables
- $45$ pieces of meat and $60$ pieces of vegetables
- $72$ pieces of meat and $96$ pieces of vegetables

choices.content: $18$ pieces of meat and $30$ pieces of vegetables
$20$ pieces of meat and $32$ pieces of vegetables
$30$ pieces of meat and $40$ pieces of vegetables
$45$ pieces of meat and $60$ pieces of vegetables
$72$ pieces of meat and $96$ pieces of vegetables

assessment_items.question_content:
Makoto likes to eat her trail mix piece by piece. For every $5$ pieces of nuts she eats, she eats $2$ pieces of candy.

**Which of the following could be what Makoto eats from a bag of trail mix?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The ratio of the pieces of nuts to the pieces of candy Makoto eats is $5:2$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Nuts | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$ | $10$
Candy | $2$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$  | $4$

If she eats $2$ times as many pieces of nuts, then she must eat $2$ times as many pieces of candy.

$10:4$ is equivalent to $5:2$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Nuts | $5$ | $\stackrel{\times 9}\rightarrow$ | $45$
Candy | $2$ | $\stackrel{\times 9}\rightarrow$  | $18$

If she eats $9$ times as many pieces of nuts, then she must eat $9$ times as many pieces of candy.

$45:18$ is equivalent to $5:2$.

Makoto could eat the following from a bag of trail mix:

- $10$ pieces of nuts and $4$ pieces of candy
- $45$ pieces of nuts and $18$ pieces of candy

choices.content: $10$ pieces of nuts and $4$ pieces of candy
$15$ pieces of nuts and $12$ pieces of candy
$25$ pieces of nuts and $20$ pieces of candy
$40$ pieces of nuts and $18$ pieces of candy
$45$ pieces of nuts and $18$ pieces of candy

assessment_items.question_content:
Tomas needs exactly $6$ potatoes to make $12$ potato pancakes.

**Which of the following statements are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The ratio of potatoes to potato pancakes is $6:12$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Potatoes | $6$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$ | $12$
Pancakes | $12$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$  | $24$

If Tomas uses $2$ times as many potatoes, then he can make $2$ times as many potato pancakes.

$12:24$ is equivalent to $6:12$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Potatoes | $6$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$ | $18$
Pancakes | $12$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$  | $36$

If Tomas uses $3$ times as many potatoes, then he can make $3$ times as many potato pancakes.

$18:36$ is equivalent to $6:12$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Potatoes | $6$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$ | $30$
Pancakes | $12$ | $\stackrel{\times 5}\rightarrow$  | $60$

If Tomas uses $5$ times as many potatoes, then he can make $5$ times as many potato pancakes.

$30:60$ is equivalent to $6:12$.

The following statements are true:

- Tomas needs exactly $12$ potatoes to make $24$ potato pancakes.
- Tomas needs exactly $18$ potatoes to make $36$ potato pancakes.
- Tomas needs exactly $30$ potatoes to make $60$ potato pancakes.

choices.content: Tomas needs exactly $12$ potatoes to make $24$ potato pancakes.
Tomas needs exactly $18$ potatoes to make $36$ potato pancakes.
Tomas needs exactly $24$ potatoes to make $44$ potato pancakes.
Tomas needs exactly $30$ potatoes to make $60$ potato pancakes.
Tomas needs exactly $36$ potatoes to make $78$ potato pancakes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Peter is a beekeeper who sells jars of honey and honeycombs at the market. For every $10$ jars of honey he brings to the market, he brings $3$ honeycombs.

**Which of the following could be what Peter brings to the market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The ratio of jars of honey to number of honeycombs Peter brings to the market is $10:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Jars | $10$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$ | $20$
Honeycombs | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$  | $6$

If Peter brings $2$ times as many jars of honey to the market, then he needs to bring $2$ times as many honeycombs as well. 

$20:6$ is equivalent to $10:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Jars | $10$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$ | $30$
Honeycombs | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 3}\rightarrow$  | $9$

If Peter brings $3$ times as many jars of honey to the market, then he needs to bring $3$ times as many honeycombs as well. 

$30:9$ is equivalent to $10:3$.

The following could be what Peter brings to the market:

- $20$ jars of honey and $6$ honeycombs
- $30$ jars of honey and $9$ honeycombs

choices.content: $18$ jars of honey and $5$ honeycombs
$20$ jars of honey and $6$ honeycombs
$30$ jars of honey and $9$ honeycombs
$40$ jars of honey and $10$ honeycombs
$60$ jars of honey and $16$ honeycombs

assessment_items.question_content:
For every $3$ days of vacation, Farah packs $4$ outfits.

**Which of the following statements are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: For Farah, the ratio of the number of outfits to the length of the vacation in days is $4:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Outfits | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$ | $8$
Days | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 2}\rightarrow$  | $6$

If Farah goes on a vacation that is $2$ times as long, then she would pack $2$ times as many outfits.

$8:6$ is equivalent to $4:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Outfits | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 4}\rightarrow$ | $16$
Days | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 4}\rightarrow$  | $12$

If Farah goes on a vacation that is $4$ times as long, then she would pack $4$ times as many outfits.

$16:12$ is equivalent to $4:3$.

|||
-|-|-|-
Outfits | $4$ | $\stackrel{\times 7}\rightarrow$ | $28$
Days | $3$ | $\stackrel{\times 7}\rightarrow$  | $21$

If Farah goes on a vacation that is $7$ times as long, then she would pack $7$ times as many outfits.

$28:21$ is equivalent to $4:3$.

The following statements are true:

- Farah would pack $8$ outfits for a $6$-day vacation.
- Farah would pack $16$ outfits for a $12$-day vacation.
- Farah would pack $28$ outfits for a $21$-day vacation.

choices.content: Farah would pack $6$ outfits for an $8$-day vacation.
Farah would pack $8$ outfits for a $6$-day vacation.
Farah would pack $10$ outfits for a $9$-day vacation.
Farah would pack $16$ outfits for a $12$-day vacation.
Farah would pack $28$ outfits for a $21$-day vacation.
